title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied, 40, from Reading, was sentenced for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts, including leopard and orangutan skulls.
Bied received an eight-month prison sentence and a $75,000 fine for conspiracy to smuggle goods and violating the Lacey Act.
He imported animal parts from Cameroon and Indonesia, falsely labeling them to evade U.S. Fish and Wildlife Services detection.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black, a Taunton man, was convicted of first-degree murder for the fatal stabbing of Milteer Hendricks in 2023.
Black and four others attacked unarmed 19-year-old Hendricks outside an apartment in Falmouth, according to prosecutors.
Black's conviction carries a mandatory life sentence without parole; sentencing is scheduled for Friday.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia of Massachusetts State Police was suspended with pay after being named in an investigative report.
DeLucia allegedly urged concealing information regarding Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged DUI crash that resulted in a death.
Sgt. Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide from a 2023 crash; his law enforcement certification was revoked.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adams couple sentenced to 3-5 years for manslaughter in the 2020 death of their 10-month-old foster child.
Child died from untreated medical issues; couple ignored urgent signs of necessary medical care, says DA's office.
Sentences staggered due to couple's four other children; both barred from future caregiving roles outside their family.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was sentenced to life without parole for poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze.
Church was convicted of first-degree murder after Fowler's death from ethylene glycol poisoning in 2022.
Prosecutors described the relationship as toxic, citing jealousy and alleged past threats by Church.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox have started the 2026 season with a 1-5 record after a five-game losing streak.
Boston's pitching rotation struggles with a 5.22 ERA, ranking last in MLB for home runs allowed per nine innings.
The Red Sox offense is batting .208, with poor plate discipline contributing to a 29.4% strikeout rate, fourth-worst in MLB.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore announces retirement after 13 NFL seasons, having played for six teams including the Patriots and Bills.
Gilmore, 35, earned five Pro Bowl selections and won Super Bowl LIII with the Patriots in 2018.
He concluded his NFL career with 617 tackles, 149 pass deflections, and 32 interceptions, including two touchdowns.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle layoffs have started globally, potentially affecting up to 30,000 workers; scope not yet confirmed.
Oracle's Massachusetts and New Hampshire offices have not reported layoffs in state filings as of March 27.
A $2.1 billion restructuring plan, filed in September 2025, may relate to Oracle's recent layoffs.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year deal with the Cincinnati Bengals, marking his third team in three years.
Dugger played nine games for the Steelers last year, recording 42 tackles, two interceptions, and a sack.
The former Patriots captain spent five-and-a-half seasons with New England before joining the Steelers and now the Bengals.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes's truck was stolen from Logan Airport's central parking while his family returned from vacation.
Massachusetts State Police found similarities between Byrnes’s theft and a previous truck theft in the same garage.
The truck was last tracked to New Jersey; no arrests have been made, and investigations are ongoing.
